other_observers: Brandon Percival; I found and identified the bird
duration: off and on for about 5 minutes over two hours or so
specific_location: east end of Pueblo Res. near dam
description: A small gull, smaller than neighboring Ring-billed Gulls (RBGU), the bird was very well marked. It's head was heavily smudged with medium brown, forming a well-demarcated hood. The head was small and quite rounded. The eyes were dark and the bill was short, thin, and entirely yellow, but for a tiny smudge near the tip.  The mantle and upperside to the wings were a medium gray, obviously a shade or two darker than those of the nearby RBGUs, but just a bit paler than those of a nearby californica California Gull (CAGU). The belly was white.  The bird's upper surface while swimming was angled up from the neck to the wingtips, more so than even were the RBGUs, creating a more-buoyant appearance than presented by the RBGUs. The wingtip projection was quite long and the wingtips crossed at about the tip of the tail.Behaviors: Swimming with other gulls (RBGU, CAGU, Herring Gull, Lesser Black-backed Gull)Call: None heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
